title: eriochloa ramosa
intro: Eriochloa ramosa, also known as the weeping wheatgrass or panic grass, is a type of perennial grass native to Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of eriochloa ramosa include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. The herbal preparation typically involves drying and infusing the leaves and stems in hot water or making it into a tea for medicinal purposes.
